title: Distributed Cache Layer
description: A Redis-inspired distributed cache with consistent hashing, TTL eviction, and a lightweight gRPC API — built to explore systems design at scale.
problem: Microservices often need shared, low-latency state without overloading a central database. Off-the-shelf solutions are powerful but opaque — I wanted to understand the tradeoffs firsthand.
approach: Designed a sharded key-value store with virtual nodes on a hash ring, replication factor of 2, and LRU eviction per shard. Exposed a minimal gRPC interface and instrumented with Prometheus metrics.
solution: The system handles 50k+ ops/sec in local benchmarks with sub-millisecond p99 reads. Shard rebalancing on node add/remove completes in under 200ms for a 4-node cluster.
learned: Consistent hashing is elegant on paper but edge cases (hot keys, uneven vnode distribution) require careful tuning. Observability from day one made debugging replication lag far easier.
